description:
During active balancing:
  · Selected cells shall be discharged through balancing resistor
  · Balancing shall continue until delta < 20 mV or timeout reached
  · Balancing shall be suspended when pack current > 5 A
supplementary_info:
Function: Cell Balancing
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring > 4.2 SOH Degradation Monitoring > 5 Cell Balancing > 5.1 Balancing Detection and Activation

analysis.direction:
triggering: delta < 20 mV, pack current > 5 A
clearing: none found
remaining: balancing continues until conditions are met
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Test the normal behavior of cell balancing where selected cells are discharged through a balancing resistor until the delta voltage is less than 20 mV or the timeout period is reached.
Test the boundary condition for the delta threshold, ensuring that the balancing stops when the delta voltage reaches exactly 20 mV.
Test the boundary condition for the pack current threshold, ensuring that the balancing is suspended when the pack current exceeds 5 A.
Test the behavior of cell balancing during a timeout event where the balancing continues until the specified timeout period is reached without any other conditions being met.
Test the state transition from active balancing to suspended balancing when the pack current exceeds 5 A.

cases.title: Test the normal behavior of cell balancing where selected cells are discharged through a balancing resistor until delta < 20 mV or timeout period is reached
cases.objective: To verify that the BMS discharges selected cells through a balancing resistor and suspends balancing when pack current exceeds 5 A, ensuring the process adheres to specified thresholds.
cases.precondition: BMS initialized, all parameters within normal operating range, no active faults. Selected cells have a delta voltage greater than 20 mV and pack current is below 5 A.
cases.postcondition: System returned to normal operating state with no active faults, selected cells are balanced within specified thresholds and pack current is below 5 A.

steps.action: Set the initial state where selected cells have a delta voltage > 20 mV and pack current < 5 A, then apply active balancing.
steps.expected: The BMS shall initiate cell balancing by discharging the selected cells through the balancing resistor. The system shall continue to monitor the delta voltage and pack current.

steps.action: Simulate a scenario where the delta voltage between selected cells decreases below 20 mV or wait for t_BalancingTimeout duration.
steps.expected: The BMS shall stop discharging through the balancing resistor when the delta voltage is less than 20 mV, and the system shall return to normal operating state. If the timeout period r_BalancingTimeout is reached without the delta voltage dropping below 20 mV, the BMS shall also stop discharging.

steps.action: Simulate a scenario where pack current exceeds 5 A during active balancing.
steps.expected: The BMS shall suspend cell balancing and cease discharging through the balancing resistor. The system shall wait for t_BalancingTimeout duration before resuming balancing if conditions allow, or return to normal operating state after the timeout period.

cases.raw_html:
<testcase>
<title>Test the normal behavior of cell balancing where selected cells are discharged through a balancing resistor until delta < 20 mV or timeout period is reached</title>
<objective>To verify that the BMS discharges selected cells through a balancing resistor and suspends balancing when pack current exceeds 5 A, ensuring the process adheres to specified thresholds.</objective>
<related_requirement>REQ-BMS-BAL-002</related_requirement>
<precondition>BMS initialized, all parameters within normal operating range, no active faults. Selected cells have a delta voltage greater than 20 mV and pack current is below 5 A.</precondition>
<steps>
<step order="1">
<action>Set the initial state where selected cells have a delta voltage > 20 mV and pack current < 5 A, then apply active balancing.</action>
<expected>The BMS shall initiate cell balancing by discharging the selected cells through the balancing resistor. The system shall continue to monitor the delta voltage and pack current.</expected>
</step>
<step order="2">
<action>Simulate a scenario where the delta voltage between selected cells decreases below 20 mV or wait for t_BalancingTimeout duration.</action>
<expected>The BMS shall stop discharging through the balancing resistor when the delta voltage is less than 20 mV, and the system shall return to normal operating state. If the timeout period r_BalancingTimeout is reached without the delta voltage dropping below 20 mV, the BMS shall also stop discharging.</expected>
</step>
<step order="3">
<action>Simulate a scenario where pack current exceeds 5 A during active balancing.</action>
<expected>The BMS shall suspend cell balancing and cease discharging through the balancing resistor. The system shall wait for t_BalancingTimeout duration before resuming balancing if conditions allow, or return to normal operating state after the timeout period.</expected>
</step>
</steps>
<postcondition>System returned to normal operating state with no active faults, selected cells are balanced within specified thresholds and pack current is below 5 A.</postcondition>
</testcase>

cases.title: Test the boundary condition for delta threshold during cell balancing
cases.objective: To verify that cell balancing stops when the delta voltage reaches exactly 20 mV.
cases.precondition: BMS initialized, all parameters within normal operating range, no active faults. Cells are overvoltage and pack current is below 5 A.
cases.postcondition: System returned to normal operating state with no active faults and cells balanced as per requirement.

steps.action: Set the delta voltage difference between selected cells to exactly 20 mV.
steps.expected: The BMS shall initiate cell balancing through the balancing resistor. [NEEDS REVIEW] Balancing shall continue until the delta voltage is below 20 mV or timeout reached, but not before.

steps.action: Wait for a sufficient time to observe the behavior of the BMS.
steps.expected: [NEEDS REVIEW] The BMS shall stop cell balancing when the delta voltage difference is below 20 mV or timeout reached, whichever occurs first. [NEEDS REVIEW] If the timeout is reached before the delta voltage drops below 20 mV, the BMS shall stop balancing.

steps.action: Verify that cell balancing has stopped.
steps.expected: The BMS shall indicate that cell balancing has been suspended due to the delta voltage difference being within acceptable limits or timeout reached. [NEEDS REVIEW] The state of the system shall return to normal operating state after the balancing is suspended.

cases.raw_html:

<title>Test the boundary condition for delta threshold during cell balancing</title>
<objective>To verify that cell balancing stops when the delta voltage reaches exactly 20 mV.</objective>
<related_requirement>REQ-BMS-BAL-002</related_requirement>
<precondition>BMS initialized, all parameters within normal operating range, no active faults. Cells are overvoltage and pack current is below 5 A.</precondition>
<steps>
<step order="1">
<action>Set the delta voltage difference between selected cells to exactly 20 mV.</action>
<expected>The BMS shall initiate cell balancing through the balancing resistor. [NEEDS REVIEW] Balancing shall continue until the delta voltage is below 20 mV or timeout reached, but not before.</expected>
</step>
<step order="2">
<action>Wait for a sufficient time to observe the behavior of the BMS.</action>
<expected>[NEEDS REVIEW] The BMS shall stop cell balancing when the delta voltage difference is below 20 mV or timeout reached, whichever occurs first. [NEEDS REVIEW] If the timeout is reached before the delta voltage drops below 20 mV, the BMS shall stop balancing.</expected>
</step>
<step order="3">
<action>Verify that cell balancing has stopped.</action>
<expected>The BMS shall indicate that cell balancing has been suspended due to the delta voltage difference being within acceptable limits or timeout reached. [NEEDS REVIEW] The state of the system shall return to normal operating state after the balancing is suspended.</expected>

<postcondition>System returned to normal operating state with no active faults and cells balanced as per requirement.</postcondition>

cases.title: Test the suspension of cell balancing when pack current exceeds 5 A
cases.objective: To verify that the BMS suspends cell balancing when the pack current exceeds the threshold of 5 A, ensuring safety and proper operation.
cases.precondition: BMS initialized, all parameters within normal operating range, no active faults. Cells are selected for balancing, delta > 20 mV, r_BalancingTimeout not reached.
cases.postcondition: BMS returns to normal operating state, cell balancing is suspended, no active faults present.

steps.action: Set the pack current to exceed the threshold of 5 A
steps.expected: The BMS shall suspend cell balancing and set a flag indicating that balancing is suspended due to high pack current. [NEEDS REVIEW] Wait for t_BalancingTimeout debounce duration.

cases.title: Test cell balancing behavior during a timeout event
cases.objective: To verify that the BMS continues cell balancing until the specified timeout period is reached without suspension due to pack current exceeding 5 A.
cases.precondition: BMS initialized, all parameters within normal operating range, no active faults. Selected cells are overvoltage and require balancing.
cases.postcondition: BMS returns to normal operating state with no active faults, selected cells are balanced within acceptable limits or the timeout period is reached without further action required.

steps.action: Set the delta threshold to a value greater than 20 mV (e.g., r_CellOV_Threshold).
steps.expected: The BMS shall initiate cell balancing.

steps.action: Simulate a timeout period of t_BalancingTimeout without any pack current exceeding 5 A or the delta threshold being met.
steps.expected: [NEEDS REVIEW] The BMS shall continue to apply the balancing resistor until the timeout period is reached.

steps.action: Wait for the timeout period t_BalancingTimeout to elapse.
steps.expected: The BMS shall stop cell balancing and return to normal operating state as per postcondition.

cases.title: Test the state transition from active balancing to suspended balancing when pack current exceeds 5 A
cases.objective: To verify that the BMS transitions from active balancing to suspended balancing when the pack current exceeds 5 A.
cases.precondition: BMS initialized, all parameters within normal operating range, no active faults. Cells are selected for balancing and delta > 20 mV.
cases.postcondition: The system returns to a normal operating state with no active faults, and the cells are in a balanced condition as per the last recorded state before suspension of balancing.

steps.action: Set the pack current to exceed 5 A (r_PackCurrent_Suspend).
steps.expected: The BMS shall initiate suspension of cell balancing due to high pack current. The state of active balancing shall transition to suspended balancing, and a warning flag indicating suspension of balancing shall be set.

steps.action: Wait for the debounce period t_CellOV_Debounce.
steps.expected: [NEEDS REVIEW] The BMS shall confirm that the state has been stable in suspended balancing for the duration of t_CellOV_Debounce. No further action is required during this step as it only involves waiting for the debounce period.

steps.action: Verify the system state after the debounce period.
steps.expected: The BMS shall maintain the suspended balancing state and no longer discharge cells through the balancing resistor. The delta between selected cells shall not decrease further, and any active balancing signals shall be deactivated.

description:
Upon charger connection detection (CP/PP or CC signal):
  · Verify all safety preconditions
  · IF all preconditions satisfied
    THEN BMS_ChargeReady_Flag := 1
  · ELSE BMS_ChargeReady_Flag := 0, report reason on CAN
supplementary_info:
Function: Charge Management
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring > 4.2 SOH Degradation Monitoring > 5 Cell Balancing > 5.1 Balancing Detection and Activation > 6 Insulation and HVIL > 6.1 Insulation Resistance Monitoring > 6.2 HV Interlock Monitoring > 6.3 HV Leakage Detection > 7 Communication and Diagnostics > 7.1 CAN Bus Fault > 7.2 VCU Heartbeat Monitoring > 8 Undervoltage Protection > 8.1 Detection and Response > 9 Charge Management > 9.1 Charge Initiation
Context: Charge initiation is gated by a set of safety preconditions verified after charger connection. The BMS signals readiness on CAN once all preconditions are satisfied. If any precondition is not met, the reason is reported on CAN for diagnostic and HMI purposes:
- BMS_ChargeReady_Flag = 1: charger detected, no active faults, temperature in range, pack voltage in range
- Preconditions checked: active faults (sections 1-8), temperature limits (section 3), OVP state (section 1), undervoltage (section 8)

analysis.direction: triggering: BMS_ChargeReady_Flag := 1 if all preconditions are satisfied; BMS_ChargeReady_Flag := 0 if any precondition is not met.
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Test the normal behavior of setting BMS_ChargeReady_Flag to 1 when all safety preconditions are satisfied upon charger connection.
Test the scenario where a fault is detected and BMS_ChargeReady_Flag remains at 0 with the reason reported on CAN.
Test the boundary condition of temperature limits, ensuring that BMS_ChargeReady_Flag does not change when operating within or just outside the temperature range.
Verify the state transition from BMS_ChargeReady_Flag = 0 to BMS_ChargeReady_Flag = 1 as preconditions are met during charger connection.
Test that the reason for BMS_ChargeReady_Flag being set to 0 is correctly reported on CAN when a fault condition exists.

description:
While BMS_CellOT_Flag = 1 AND charging:
  · IF cell temperature > 45 C
    THEN BMS_ChargeDerating_Active := 1
  · Charge current shall be reduced proportionally to temperature rise
supplementary_info:
Function: Thermal Management
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection

analysis.direction: triggering: BMS_ChargeDerating_Active := 1 when cell temperature > 45 C and BMS_CellOT_Flag = 1 AND charging.
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Test the normal behavior where BMS_CellOT_Flag is active, cell temperature exceeds 45 C during charging, and BMS_ChargeDerating_Active is set to 1.
Test the boundary condition where cell temperature is exactly at 45 C while BMS_CellOT_Flag is active and charging, ensuring no false triggering of BMS_ChargeDerating_Active.
Test a scenario where BMS_CellOT_Flag is active but cell temperature does not exceed 45 C during charging to ensure that BMS_ChargeDerating_Active remains inactive.
Test the state transition from normal operation to derating mode when BMS_CellOT_Flag transitions from 0 to 1 and cell temperature exceeds 45 C during charging.

cases.title: Test BMS behavior when BMS_CellOT_Flag is active, cell temperature exceeds 45 C during charging
cases.objective: To verify that the BMS reduces charge current proportionally to temperature rise and sets BMS_ChargeDerating_Active to 1 when BMS_CellOT_Flag = 1 AND cell_temperature > r_CellOV_Threshold during charging.
cases.precondition: BMS initialized, all parameters within normal operating range, no active faults. Cell temperature is below 45 C and BMS_CellOT_FlagBMS_ChargeDerating_Active are both 0.
cases.postcondition: BMS returned to normal operating state.

steps.action: Set cell_temperature to r_CellOV_Threshold + 1 (symbolic value for 46 C).
steps.expected: BMS_CellOT_Flag := 1; BMS_ChargeDerating_Active := 1

steps.action: Apply charging current.
steps.expected: BMS_ChargeDerating_Active signal is updated to reflect the proportional reduction in charge current based on cell_temperature increase. [NEEDS REVIEW]

steps.action: Wait for t_CellOV_Debounce duration (symbolic value).
steps.expected: BMS returns to normal operating state with BMS_ChargeDerating_Active := 0 and BMS_CellOT_Flag := 0. [NEEDS REVIEW]

description:
IF  any cell voltage <= 2.80 V
THEN
  · BMS_CellUV_Flag := 1
  · Limit discharge power
supplementary_info:
Function: Undervoltage Protection
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring > 4.2 SOH Degradation Monitoring > 5 Cell Balancing > 5.1 Balancing Detection and Activation > 6 Insulation and HVIL > 6.1 Insulation Resistance Monitoring > 6.2 HV Interlock Monitoring > 6.3 HV Leakage Detection > 7 Communication and Diagnostics > 7.1 CAN Bus Fault > 7.2 VCU Heartbeat Monitoring > 8 Undervoltage Protection > 8.1 Detection and Response
Context: Undervoltage protection prevents cell damage from over-discharge by limiting power draw and, if the voltage continues to fall, opening the discharge contactor. A proportional power limit is applied between the warning and critical thresholds to give the load time to reduce demand before hard shutdown. Recovery requires cells to return above 3.00 V:
- BMS_CellUV_Flag = 1: any cell <= 2.80 V (proportional power limiting)
- BMS_CellUV_L2_Flag = 1: any cell <= 2.50 V (contactor open, discharge prohibited)

analysis.direction: triggering: BMS_CellUV_Flag := 1 when any cell voltage <= 2.80 V; clearing: BMS_CellUV_Flag := 0 when cells return above 3.00 V.
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Test the normal behavior where a cell voltage is at or below the threshold of 2.80 V, causing BMS_CellUV_Flag to be set to 1 and power limiting.
Test the boundary condition where a cell voltage is exactly at 2.80 V, ensuring that BMS_CellUV_Flag is set to 1.
Test the fault condition where a cell voltage is below the lower threshold of 2.50 V, causing both BMS_CellUV_Flag and BMS_CellUV_L2_Flag to be set to 1 and the discharge contactor to open.
Test the state transition from normal operation to undervoltage protection mode when a cell voltage drops below 2.80 V, and back to normal operation when cells return above 3.00 V.
Verify that BMS_CellUV_Flag is correctly observed and reported by the system when any cell voltage falls below 2.80 V.

description:
The BMS shall continuously track:
  · Capacity fade (current vs. rated capacity)
  · DC internal resistance trend
  · Cell voltage deviation under load
supplementary_info:
Function: Degradation Monitoring
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring > 4.2 SOH Degradation Monitoring > 5 Cell Balancing > 5.1 Balancing Detection and Activation > 6 Insulation and HVIL > 6.1 Insulation Resistance Monitoring > 6.2 HV Interlock Monitoring > 6.3 HV Leakage Detection > 7 Communication and Diagnostics > 7.1 CAN Bus Fault > 7.2 VCU Heartbeat Monitoring > 8 Undervoltage Protection > 8.1 Detection and Response > 9 Charge Management > 9.1 Charge Initiation > 9.2 Charge Termination > 10 Pack Voltage Protection > 10.1 Pack Voltage Monitoring > 11 Degradation Monitoring > 11.1 Cell Degradation Detection
Context: Degradation monitoring tracks long-term cell aging through multiple indicators to detect capacity loss and impedance increase before they affect system performance. The SOH prediction model is updated when any indicator exceeds its threshold:
- Capacity fade: current capacity vs. rated capacity
- Impedance growth: DC internal resistance trend
- Voltage deviation: cell voltage dispersion under load
- BMS_CellDegrade_Warn = 1: any indicator exceeds defined threshold

analysis.direction: triggering: BMS_CellDegrade_Warn = 1 when any indicator exceeds its threshold.

description:
Upon VCU_HVDisconnect_Req or critical fault:
  · Open both main contactors
  · Discharge DC link capacitance
  · BMS_SystemState := HV_Deactivating
  · THEN Standby or SafeState as appropriate
supplementary_info:
Function: BMS State Machine
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring > 4.2 SOH Degradation Monitoring > 5 Cell Balancing > 5.1 Balancing Detection and Activation > 6 Insulation and HVIL > 6.1 Insulation Resistance Monitoring > 6.2 HV Interlock Monitoring > 6.3 HV Leakage Detection > 7 Communication and Diagnostics > 7.1 CAN Bus Fault > 7.2 VCU Heartbeat Monitoring > 8 Undervoltage Protection > 8.1 Detection and Response > 9 Charge Management > 9.1 Charge Initiation > 9.2 Charge Termination > 10 Pack Voltage Protection > 10.1 Pack Voltage Monitoring > 11 Degradation Monitoring > 11.1 Cell Degradation Detection > 12 Fault Management > 12.1 Unrecoverable Fault Handling > 13 BMS State Machine > 13.1 Power-Up and Initialization > 13.2 HV Activation > 13.3 HV Deactivation and Sleep
Context: HV deactivation is triggered by normal shutdown requests or fault conditions from any protection function (sections 1-12). After contactors open and the DC link discharges, the system may enter low-power sleep mode to conserve quiescent current while maintaining wake-up capability:
- Deactivation: open contactors, discharge DC link, transition through HV_Deactivating to Standby or SafeState
- Sleep entry: Standby with no CAN activity for configurable timeout
- Sleep exit: CAN activity or wake pin assertion

analysis.direction: The requirement describes a triggering behavior: the system transitions to HV_Deactivating and then to Standby or SafeState as appropriate upon VCU_HVDisconnect_Req or critical fault.
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Test the normal behavior of transitioning from BMS_SystemState to HV_Deactivating and then to Standby or SafeState upon VCU_HVDisconnect_Req.

Test that the system correctly handles HV deactivation and sleep entry after contactors open and DC link discharges, even with no CAN activity for a configurable timeout period.
Ensure the system properly transitions from Standby to SafeState under fault conditions.

<step order="2">
<action>Wait for the debounce period (t_CellOV_Debounce) to elapse after CAN communication is restored and fault cleared.</action>
<expected>BMS_CANFault_Flag transitions from 1 to 0 [NEEDS REVIEW] due to debounce period not specified</expected>

<step order="3">

</step>

description:
IF  calculated SOH <= 80 %
THEN
  · BMS_BatteryDegrade_Warn := 1
  · Store DTC P0A0F in non-volatile memory
supplementary_info:
Function: SOC/SOH Estimation
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring > 4.2 SOH Degradation Monitoring
Context: SOH degradation tracking enables early detection of battery aging and supports warranty analysis. Degradation is evaluated through multiple indicators including capacity fade and cumulative throughput. When the design cycle limit is exceeded, an SOH re-calibration is requested:
- BMS_BatteryDegrade_Warn = 1: SOH <= 80 %
- BMS_SOHEstFault_Flag = 1: cumulative throughput exceeds limit, DTC P0A0F stored

analysis.direction: triggering: BMS_BatteryDegrade_Warn := 1 when SOH <= 80 %
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Test the normal behavior where calculated SOH is exactly 80% and BMS_BatteryDegrade_Warn should be set to 1.
Test the boundary condition where calculated SOH is slightly less than 80%, ensuring BMS_BatteryDegrade_Warn remains at 0.
Verify that DTC P0A0F is stored in non-volatile memory when BMS_BatteryDegrade_Warn is set to 1 due to SOH <= 80%.
Test the normal behavior where calculated SOH is significantly less than 80%, ensuring BMS_BatteryDegrade_Warn remains at 1 consistently.
Simulate a scenario where cumulative throughput exceeds the limit, and verify that DTC P0A0F is stored in non-volatile memory without affecting BMS_BatteryDegrade_Warn state.

cases.postcondition: The system returns to a normal operating state with BMS_BatteryDegrade_Warn flag set and DTC P0A0F stored in non-volatile memory.

description:
IF  pack discharge current >= 300 A for >= 10 ms
THEN
  · BMS_SCD_Flag := 1
  · Open both main contactors immediately
  · BMS_SystemState := SafeState
supplementary_info:
Function: Current Management
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection
Context: Short circuit detection uses a higher threshold and shorter integration window than overcurrent detection, targeting hard faults such as harness shorts or inverter bridge shoot-through. The SCD response overrides all other current management actions and requires a diagnostic clear to recover:
- BMS_SCD_Flag = 1: discharge current >= 300 A for >= 10 ms
- BMS_SCD_Latch = 1: latched state persistent across power cycles

analysis.direction: triggering
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Test the normal behavior where the discharge current is exactly 300 A for exactly 10 ms, causing BMS_SCD_Flag to be set and both main contactors to open.
Test the boundary condition where the discharge current is slightly above 300 A (e.g., 300.1 A) for exactly 10 ms, ensuring BMS_SCD_Flag is set and actions are taken as expected.
Test the boundary condition where the discharge current is exactly 300 A for slightly more than 10 ms (e.g., 15 ms), ensuring BMS_SCD_Flag is set and actions are taken as expected.
Test a scenario where the discharge current exceeds 300 A by a significant margin (e.g., 400 A) for exactly 10 ms, to ensure robustness of the detection logic.
Test the state transition from normal operation to SafeState after BMS_SCD_Flag is set and both main contactors are opened.

cases.title: Test the BMS_SCD_Flag setting and main contactor opening when pack discharge current is exactly 300 A for exactly 10 ms

cases.precondition: System state before the test begins: BMS initialized, all parameters within normal operating range, no active faults.
cases.postcondition: System state after the test completes: System returned to normal operating state, with BMS_SCD_Flag reset and all systems operational.

description:
IF  BMS_ChargeTerm_Req = 1 AND any cell voltage >= r_CellOV_L3_Threshold
THEN
  · BMS_CellOV_L3_Flag := 1
supplementary_info:
Function: Overvoltage Protection
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery
Context: If the overvoltage condition escalates beyond Level 2 despite derating, immediate contactor opening is required to protect the battery. Contactors are opened in sequence and the system transitions to a safe state:
- BMS_ChargeContactor_State: 0 = Open, 1 = Closed
- BMS_DischargeContactor_State: 0 = Open, 1 = Closed
- BMS_SystemState transitions to SafeState; refer to section 13 for state definitions
- Recovery requires all cell voltages below 4.00 V for >= 5 s

description:
IF  any cell temperature <= low temperature limit
THEN
  · BMS_CellUT_Flag := 1
  · BMS_ChargeReady_Flag := 0 (charging prohibited)
supplementary_info:
Function: Thermal Management
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection
Context: Low temperature protection prevents charging when cells are too cold to accept current without damage, and manages the battery heater to enable charging under cold conditions. The heater is de-energized on overtemperature or when no longer needed:
- BMS_CellUT_Flag = 1: any cell <= low temperature limit (charging prohibited, BMS_ChargeReady_Flag = 0)
- BMS_HeaterOT_Flag = 1: heater temperature exceeds safe limit
- Heater activation threshold: cell <= -10 C during charge session

analysis.direction: triggering: BMS_CellUT_Flag := 1 when any cell temperature <= low temperature limit; clearing: BMS_ChargeReady_Flag := 0 (charging prohibited)
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Test the normal behavior where a single cell temperature is at or below the low temperature limit, causing BMS_CellUT_Flag to be set and BMS_ChargeReady_Flag to be cleared.
Test the boundary condition where exactly one cell temperature is at the low temperature limit threshold, ensuring that BMS_CellUT_Flag is triggered while other cells are above the limit.
Test the scenario where multiple cells are below the low temperature limit to ensure all BMS_CellUT_Flag instances trigger and BMS_ChargeReady_Flag remains cleared.
Test the state transition from normal operation to under-temperature protection mode, including heater activation if necessary.
Verify that the system correctly resets BMS_CellUT_Flag and BMS_ChargeReady_Flag when all cell temperatures rise above the low temperature limit threshold.
